messages.text: How can I help with your doc?
write a prd for this project

I have compiled a comprehensive, enterprise-ready **Product Requirement Document (PRD) for Project Ollie** based on the architectural, user experience, and design notes from your reference files (including the Ollie Eng HLD, the daily UX daily standups, and the Daniel/Zhengrong sync notes). 

This PRD unifies the technical specifications of Ollie's backend services (Task, Feed, Workstream, Memory, Sobi, and Jetski) with the minimalist UX philosophy, two-stage HITL governance, and robust privacy controls discussed by the team.

Let me know if you would like me to adjust the target release timelines, add specific key performance indicators (KPIs), or further expand on the integration with tools like CodeMender or Wiz!

sandboxFiles.name: [NTK] Project Ollie Eng Design
[UX Research plan] Project Ollie: Baseline Workflow & Agent Delegation
sandboxFiles.content:
Core Objective
To map the data trails, tool ecosystems, and context-sharing habits of knowledge workers in the real world, as well as their desires and expectations regarding AI delegation.
Participants
* Location & Availability: Must currently live in the U.S. and be able to attend a 90-minute, in-person study session at the NYC Google Office between July 6th and July 24th with their own laptop.1
* Company Profile: Employed at a private educational institute or a commercial business/organization (excluding public education, nonprofits, and government).1
* Software & Tool Usage:
   * Their company must use Google Workspace (Gmail, Drive, Docs, or Calendar) with official work email addresses.1
   * The "actual work" they do must involve co-editing shared documents and spreadsheets (such as Google Docs or Sheets). Users who primarily manage tasks in dedicated project management tools (like Jira, Asana, or Trello) are disqualified.1
* Role & Authority: Must have a role in their business's decision to purchase digital tools and software (either as a sole decision-maker, part of a collective group, or as an influencer).1
* Collaboration Level: They must spend at least half or more of their work week collaborating with others on projects, tasks, or shared goals.1
Details here: Ollie current needs and painpoints 


Research Questions 
* RQ1 (The Fragmented Truth): What does a knowledge worker's true "to-do list" actually look like across formal tools (Jira, Asana) and informal channels (Slack stars, open browser tabs, mental notes)?
* RQ2 (Context Breadcrumbs): When a user moves a task forward, what specific artifacts (links, documents, specific chat sentences) represent the "proof of work" that Ollie will eventually need to ingest?
* RQ3 (The Translation Gap): How do users naturally summarize a complex day of work into a project update? What details do they filter out, and what do they consider critical?
* RQ4 (Agent Delegation Triggers): What tasks do users actually want to hand off versus what they want to keep control over? What parameters (triggers, boundaries, outputs) define a successful Sojo Agent assignment?
Session script
Artifact & Task Audit (15 mins)
* Goal: Uncover the hidden, informal task ecosystems.
* Activity: Have them open their laptop and show you their current "active stack."
* Prompts to use:
   * "Show me the 3 things you know you have to do today but aren't written down in a formal project board."
   * "Look at your open browser tabs or flagged emails right now—what tasks do those represent?"
   * "Where is the conversation happening about your top priority right now? Can you show me that Slack thread/email chain?"
The "Human Ollie" Walkthrough (25 mins)
* Goal: Treat the user's memory and screen as the database to see what Ollie should have done.
* Activity: Walk backward through their last 24–48 hours of work. Have them literally point at things on their screen.
* Prompts to use:
   * "You opened this Google Doc at 10 AM and left a comment. If Ollie was a smart assistant watching this, what is the 'core project update' it should extract from that action?"
   * "If an AI wrote an update based purely on this Slack thread you just showed me, what would it miss? What's the unwritten context here?"
   * "Look at this completed task. What were all the scattered pieces of data (spreadsheets, links, client feedback) that were required to get it done?"
AI Agents tasks (15 mins)
* Goal: Use their real data to define the scope and boundaries of the agents.
* Activity: Review the real tasks identified in Phase 1 and 2. Print out blank "Sojo Agent Job Description" cards (or use a digital whiteboard) and fill them out together.
* Prompts to use:
   * "Look at this specific task you spent an hour on yesterday. If an AI Agent could have done it, what is the exact trigger to start it? (e.g., 'When a new CSV lands in this folder' or 'When a client pings me via email')."
   * "What does the agent need access to on your laptop to successfully do this?"
   * "What is the exact output you expect? A draft email? A spreadsheet update? And where should it put it?"

sandboxFiles.name: Project Ollie PRD.doc
sandboxFiles.content:
# Product Requirement Document (PRD): Project Ollie
**Project Title:** Project Ollie — Proactive Human-Agent Collaborative Workspace
**Status:** Draft
**Target Release (Public Preview):** July 21, 2026

## 1. Executive Summary
Project Ollie is an enterprise-grade collaborative workspace application designed to unify human engineering teams and proactive AI agents within organized "Workstreams." By combining automated notification/task triage, semantic context synthesis, and sandboxed background execution, Ollie eliminates severe context switching across fragmented messaging, tracking, and coding tools. 

Unlike passive, chat-based AI assistants that require continuous prompting, Ollie introduces proactive agents as first-class, authenticated team members that execute asynchronous background work under a strict "Human-in-the-Loop" (HITL) safety framework.

## 2. Background & Problem Statement
Modern software engineering and program management teams operate in highly fragmented environments, splitting attention across Google Chat, Gmail, Buganizer, Jira, Critique, and Google Docs. Knowledge workers spend up to 30% of their working hours manually:
- Triaging incoming notifications and bug reports.
- Gathering context across disconnected documents and emails.
- Drafting routine communication or scaffolding repetitive code patches.

Current generative AI tools are primarily passive and session-bound. They lack an organic understanding of a team’s persistent memory, cannot perform background execution, and run the risk of leaking sensitive enterprise IP when shared in open group environments.

## 3. Product Vision & Core Pillars
Ollie’s vision is to build a "self-driving workspace" that balances proactive autonomy with absolute human control.

### 3.1 Core Pillars
- **Centralized Context (The Org Brain):** Maintain a persistent, multi-tenant memory boundary across Personal, Workstream, Team, and Global scopes.
- **Proactive Background Execution:** Run asynchronous agents to prepare code fixes, draft emails, and flag duplicates before the user manually initiates a request.
- **Human-in-the-Loop (HITL) Governance:** Enforce explicit human review at both the start (Top Refinement) and end (Bottom Sign-off) of any agent task.
- **Minimalist, Low-Friction UX:** Prioritize visual interaction models (such as drag-and-drop mechanics and pre-drafted one-click actions) to eliminate typing-heavy prompting.

## 4. User Personas
- **The Engineer (IC):** Needs to focus on complex coding tasks. Wants Ollie to safely scaffold routine patches in a sandbox, flag duplicate bug tickets, and prepare draft code reviews.
- **The Tech Lead / Program Manager:** Needs high-level project tracking and coordination. Wants Ollie to summarize multi-channel threads, triage incoming workstreams, and draft external status updates.
- **The Security & Compliance Officer:** Demands absolute guarantees that sensitive data/IP will not leak, that agents execute with delegated human permissions, and that no unapproved code is pushed to production.

## 5. Functional Requirements & Key Features

### 5.1 Workstreams & Shared Team Context
Workstreams are the primary organizational unit in Ollie, representing a dedicated space for a project, team, or function.
- **Asset Synchronization:** Workstreams automatically ingest and map contextual assets, including Google Groups, Shared Drives, Buganizer Hotlists, and Chat spaces.
- **Organizational Brain (Memory DB):** Maintain semantic vector indexing for technical standards, team guidelines, and document history. Memory is physically partitioned to prevent data leakage between workstreams.

### 5.2 Automated Task Ingestion & Intelligent Triage
- **Multi-Channel Ingestion:** The platform runs listeners that parse real-time push events (Gmail, Google Chat webhooks) and periodic pull updates (Buganizer, Jira, Critique).
- **Workstream Auto-Routing:** When a raw task or bug is ingested, Ollie runs a two-stage classification pipeline (heuristics followed by semantic cosine similarity) to route the task to the correct Workstream.
- **Semantic Duplicate Detection:** Identify and automatically merge duplicate incoming bug tickets or requests utilizing vector embeddings.

### 5.3 Proactive Agent Execution (Sobi & Jetski)
Tasks are executed by specialized agent harnesses:
- **Sobi (Cognitive & Workspace Agent):** Executes reasoning loops using Workspace Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers to read, synthesize, and draft emails, document outlines, and chat responses.
- **Jetski (Technical & Code Execution Agent):** Executes technical workflows (such as code modifications or building steps) inside hermetically isolated user-owned Cloudtops or Container Capsules.

### 5.4 Two-Stage HITL Governance Model
To prevent runaway automation, Ollie implements a dual-gate governance model:
- **Top Refinement (Input Gate):** When an agent infers a complex or ambiguous task, it must present a scoped plan (e.g., `PLAN.md`) to the user for approval before executing any tools.
- **Bottom Sign-off (Output Gate):** Compiled code diffs, generated documents, and outbound email drafts must be explicitly approved by a human teammate before final dispatch.

### 5.5 Minimalist UI/UX Design & Personal Dashboard
- **No Prompting Workspace:** Users should rarely have to type manual prompts. Instead, tasks in the inbox display pre-compiled "one-click" drafts (e.g., "Email Julie regarding [X] is drafted. [Click to Send]").
- **Widget-Based Dashboard:** A highly customizable dashboard architecture featuring drag-and-drop widgets to show project timelines, active task queues, and automated activity streams.
- **Draft Mode Visual Safety:** Clearly isolate personal agent workspaces from shared group spaces. All draft work generated by agents remains in a visual "Draft Mode" (only visible to the task owner) until published.

## 6. Privacy, Sharing, & Security Architecture
Security is a foundational product requirement, particularly when deploying proactive agents within collaborative spaces.

### 6.1 Dual Identity Execution
- **Personal Scope:** Actions taken on personal tasks execute strictly under the user's individual session token. Workstream agents and other users have zero visibility.
- **Shared Scope:** Collaborative tasks execute under the Workstream Service Account identity, but access is validated using delegated RBAC check-ups to verify that the initiating user has read/write permissions to the underlying data sources.

### 6.2 Data Isolation & Minimal Retention
- **Local Control:** Source code, prompts, and generated patches are processed dynamically and stored locally or on client-controlled infrastructure. 
- **Zero Training Leakage:** No customer data, enterprise source code, or conversational inputs are used for model training.

## 7. Non-Functional Requirements
- **Latency:** Task auto-triage and duplicate detection must resolve within 2 seconds of event ingestion.
- **Integrations:** Smooth interoperability with development environments (including terminal CLI hooks) and Google Workspace productivity suites.
- **Compliance:** Full support for VPC Service Controls (VPC-SC) at Public Preview. Customer-Managed Encryption Keys (CMEK) and Access Transparency will be deployed as fast-follows.

## 8. Release & Testing Phased Plan
- **Phase 1: Private Preview (Closed Alpha):** Active testing with a maximum of 10 enterprise customers to validate ingestion latency, safety boundaries, and the baseline UX visual model.
- **Phase 2: Public Preview (Target: July 2026):** Open to general enterprise tier customers with global endpoints, featuring default support for Gemini 3.5 Flash and Gemini 3.1 Pro models.
- **Phase 3: General Availability (GA):** Release of advanced sovereign control integrations, support for ultra-capable model classes (e.g., Gemini 3.5 Pro, Opus), and deep integrations with 3P security compliance platforms.
